notes: KNOWN_FAILING: the pi adapter has no task-estimate scan (no core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/taskestimate.go; pi/parser.go never sets ev.TaskEstimate), and the pi driver lacks a seed_instruction_file step to install the marker rule into <cwd>/AGENTS.md — so a recording produces NO task_estimate today. The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior: the canonical lifecycle arc (ready→working→ready across a multi-turn task) plus the working-state metric vector. The task_estimate/task_completion_eta burndown the scenario centers on is NOT expressible in the phase DSL (no metric-form invariant for task_estimate, same limitation as basic-turn's TotalTokens>0); it is asserted in the recipe's verify[] and confirmed by the per-adapter parser unit tests once the pi scan lands. The interactive recipe runs 4 driven turns so completed_rounds climbs 0/4→4/4 and the eta burns down; the session must outlive the final turn by ≥10s so the post-turn settle to ready is captured. observations assert pi's model + nonzero cost/tokens over the run.

text: Interactive pi REPL launches in the seeded workspace and appears ready
First prompt (plan + emit 0/4 marker) flips the session to working
First turn completes (initial marker emitted) → back to ready
A later driven turn (advancing toward 4/4) flips back to working
Task finishes (4/4) and the session settles to ready for ≥10s; state never entered waiting — final state ready
pi REPL exits at end of run — process_exited on the session
